---
title: "Energy flux and stability across a natural stream temperature gradient"
author: "Nate Heili"
date: "2025-11-19"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Using energetic food web models to assess ecological stability
Ecologists have long sought to understand how ecosystems respond to disturbance. Theory and empirical work increasingly show that interaction strengths in complex communities are not random; instead, their organization can reveal not only how energy and materials flow through a system, but also how that system responds to perturbations. Stability can be quantified in numerous ways and has evolved through several major conceptual shifts since May (1973).

In this tutorial, I use empirical data from a broad stream thermal gradient (Junker et al. 2024) to construct Jacobian matrices using the fluxweb package (Gauzens et al. 2019).

```{r}
library(fluxweb)
library(tidyverse)
```
